the fraction $\frac{100!}{10^{50}}$ is a division of 100 factorial by 10 raised to the 50th power. this means that the denominator of the irreducible fraction will be the product of the prime factors of 10^{50} that are not canceled out by the numerator. since 10 is 2 times 5, and 10^{50} is (2*5)^{50}, the denominator will have 2^{50} and 5^{50}. however, the numerator 100! has more than enough factors of 2 and 5 to cancel out the 10^{50} in the denominator. the remaining factors in the denominator will be the factors of 10^{50} that are not canceled out by the numerator. the denominator of the irreducible fraction will be 2^{50} * 5^{50} divided by the factors of 2 and 5 that are canceled out by the numerator. the number of factors of 2 in 100! is 50, and the number of factors of 5 in 100! is 24. since 10^{50} has 50 factors of 5, the denominator will have 5^{50 - 24} = 5^{26}. the denominator will also have 2^{50 - 50} = 2^0 = 1. therefore, the denominator of the irreducible fraction is 5^{26}. *** 

the denominator of the irreducible fraction is 5^{26}. the number of factors of 5 in 100! is 24, which is less than 50. this means that the denominator will have 5^{50 - 24} = 5^{26}. the number of factors of 2 in 100! is 50, which is equal to the number of factors of 2 in 10^{50}. this means that the denominator will have 2^{50 - 50} = 2^0 = 1. therefore, the denominator of the irreducible fraction is 5^{26}. ***
